root_motivation: An LLM calling mqo-mcp-server should receive a complete, grounded BI asset (title, description, chart spec, caveats) in one tool call rather than having to orchestrate three separate tools and hand-thread their JSON contracts.
user_persona: An LLM client (Claude, GPT-5, etc.) that has just received a query_multidimensional response and wants to hand the user a ready-to-render, caveated chart without additional reasoning steps.

acceptance_criteria.description: A revenue-by-year response yields title 'Revenue by Year', a line vega_spec, and a profile_summary listing Revenue (measure) and Year (dimension).
description is a single templated sentence matching the chosen mark (e.g. 'Sum of Revenue across Year.' for an aggregating line/bar).
A semi_additive measure over a temporal axis produces a caveat naming the aggregation risk.
A high-cardinality nominal axis (> 25 distinct values) produces a clutter caveat.
A clean case (additive measure, low-cardinality nominal dimension) produces caveats: [] (empty array).
The vega_spec embedded in the bundle is a valid VL5 spec (has $schema, mark, encoding).
A malformed response (no rows / no bound) returns a structured error object, not a panic, with a nonzero exit code.

scope: Compose mqo-result-profiler + mqo-chart-recommender + mqo-vega-emitter as path-dep libs
Synthesize title from bound measure/dimension labels in the catalog
Synthesize description from the recommended mark and field labels
Emit caveats: semi_additive over temporal axis, is_calc percentage in stacked bar, cardinality > 25 clutter
Produce profile_summary from ResultProfile (row_count, measure labels, dimension labels)
Emit bi-asset.v1 JSON bundle on stdout
Return structured error object (not panic) and nonzero exit on malformed input
non_goals: No rendering — the bundle carries a VL5 spec as text JSON; no PNG/SVG/browser dependency
No network — pure serde/serde_json/clap; no HTTP calls
No dashboard composition — that is mqo-dashboard-composer (component 5)
No LLM calls — synthesis rules are deterministic template logic only
No caching or state between invocations

five_whys_trace.q: Why does this CLI exist given profiler/recommender/emitter already exist?
five_whys_trace.a: Those three each do one thing; an LLM caller must make three sequential tool calls and thread the JSON contracts manually.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is making three calls a problem?
five_whys_trace.a: Every additional hop multiplies latency, tokens, and error surface; and the intermediate contracts (result-profile.v1, chart-recommendation.v1) are internal plumbing the LLM shouldn't need to reason about.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't the LLM just thread the contracts itself?
five_whys_trace.a: Only the composite knows two things: the human title/description (needs catalog labels) and the caveats (emerge from combining profile semantics with the chosen mark). Neither can be derived by the LLM without re-implementing the profiler's label logic.

ambiguities_resolved.question: If a sibling lib is not built, does the whole build fail?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No — if a sibling lib is absent, that stage is stubbed and its ACs are deferred in the receipt rather than failing the build. The bundle's synthesis logic (title, description, caveats, profile condensation) is fully testable against fixture contracts.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is the vega_spec a string or embedded JSON object?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Embedded JSON object (not a string). The spec is a serde_json::Value serialized inline into the bi-asset.v1 bundle.
